assertions: distinct per profile and per broadcast
buildBroadcastOutboxInsert INSERT outbox ON CONFLICT (dedupe_key) DO NOTHING
chunk splits into <=batchSize preserving order no loss
buildResolveAudiencePage binds workspace_id $1 segment_id $2 ORDER BY+LIMIT/OFFSET
pagination enumerates each member once no dupes across boundaries
buildBroadcastStatusUpdate compare-and-set WHERE workspace_id=$1 AND status=fromStatus
2-member audience -> runInWorkspaceTx insert then 2 SendMessageCommands, status sending->sent
handler never throws on malformed id; SQS/EventBridge mocked
dynamic (source=evaluator) and manual (source=manual) segments both fully resolved
resolved at SEND TIME: add membership then resolve includes it; remove then excludes
audience>batchSize (50/120) -> outbox count=audience in ceil(120/50)=3 batched tx (spy)
send twice same broadcast_id same audience -> outbox unchanged (ON CONFLICT (dedupe_key) DO NOTHING)
concurrent double-invocation -> one row per profile (UNIQUE dedupe_key)
same profile different broadcast_id -> new row
scheduled stays until due then sweep flips scheduled->sending->sent
cancelled skipped by sweep no outbox rows
A and B same-shape segment overlapping emails; broadcast A resolves only A memberships, outbox only A profiles
binds workspace_id $1 so B never touched (service role bypass)
reuse dispatchOutbox (NOT re-implement): 3 members, #2 suppressed; drive dispatchOutbox over each with counting fake SES
#2 skip stoppedAt=suppression SES not called; #1,#3 send
re-dispatch same outbox ids = noop (atomic claim) -> exactly-once

mocks: counting fake SesEmailClient in chain test
fake page-reader / spy tx-runner in unit tests
NOT mocked: Postgres (real localhost:5433); NOT mocked: Dispatcher (reuse dispatchOutbox)
implUnits: services/broadcast/src/core.ts pure (dedupe key, outbox insert builder, chunk, paginate, status state machine, schedule-due, sweep query, SQS payload)

services/broadcast/src/send.ts orchestrator runBroadcast: load broadcast scoped by workspace_id from row, guard schedule-due+transition, status->sending, resolveAudience at send time, chunk, per-batch insert in runInWorkspaceTx + enqueue {outbox_id}, status->sent
services/broadcast/src/deps.ts pooled service-role reader + runStatementsInWorkspaceTx (assert values[0]=workspaceId) + injected SQS sender + now + dispatchQueueUrl
services/broadcast/src/handler.ts thin onSendNow + onScheduledSweep (EventBridge) never throws
notes: Order: core pure -> deps -> send orchestrator -> handler. Reuse don't re-implement: @cdp/segments resolveAudience (both dynamic+manual live in segment_memberships, both sources), @cdp/service-dispatcher dispatchOutbox + buildIsSuppressedQuery + parseOutboxIdFromSqsRecord ({outbox_id} contract), dispatcher runStatementsInWorkspaceTx pattern, @cdp/db testutil. Two-layered idempotency both tested: broadcast UNIQUE outbox.dedupe_key per (broadcast_id,profile_id) prevents re-enumeration double-insert on retry; dispatcher atomic claim prevents per-row double-send -> end-to-end exactly-once. 'Resolved at send time' = mutate segment_memberships after broadcast creation before send. Integration skipIf hasDatabaseUrl on real PG; SES only mocked piece in chain test; SQS/EventBridge mocked at boundary. broadcasts.audience_ref is segment_id for both segment and manual_group (manual is still a segment row) so resolveAudience covers both no branching. workspace_id loaded FROM broadcast row never client.
